channel.title: Blog on Stream Sync Engage
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Stream Sync Engage

item.title: Launching AI-powered routing
item.description:
Today we shipped AI-powered routing. Every engagement on Stream Sync Engage now passes through a routing model that decides whether the user should see it, when, and on which surface.

What changes for you

Nothing, if you do not want it to. AI routing is on by default, but every engagement can opt out with a single flag. For most teams, the right move is to let the model decide and watch what happens.

item.title: Why we built Stream Sync Engage
item.description:
Every product team we worked with had the same shape of problem. Marketing wanted to ship a survey. Product wanted to launch a guided tour. Customer success wanted a quick announcement. All three of those things should have taken an afternoon. None of them did.

What kept happening

The path was always the same. A short Slack thread about the engagement turned into a Jira ticket. The Jira ticket turned into a small frontend change. The small frontend change waited for the next release. The release got bundled with three other changes, one of which broke staging. The engagement shipped two weeks late, after the moment had passed.
